title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, pleaded not guilty to manslaughter after allegedly stabbing her boyfriend in South Boston.
Boston police identified the victim as Kyle Bradford, 26, who was pronounced dead after being transported to a hospital.
Pascual's attorney claims she acted in self-defense following threats from Bradford, who had a history of dismissed charges.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots will feature on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the first time ahead of the 2027 season.
Seattle Seahawks will appear on 'Hard Knocks' in 2026, following their Super Bowl LX win over the Patriots.
Changes in NFL rules now allow playoff teams to be featured on 'Hard Knocks' despite past restrictions.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored a goal and an assist in his recent games for the Providence Bruins.
Hagens, 19, impressed with his skating and playmaking, notably assisting on Georgii Merkulov's goal.
The Bruins' prospect could be promoted if his strong performance continues in the AHL.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots executive Eliot Wolf stated they're open to re-signing Stefon Diggs, despite his previous release due to high cap hit.
In 2025, Diggs recorded 85 catches for 1,013 yards and four touchdowns, but struggled in the postseason games.
Potential Patriots plans include Diggs as a backup option if trade talks for Eagles' A.J. Brown do not succeed.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale sentenced to 50 years for 1989 rapes and robbery in Framingham.
DNA and genealogical analysis led to Gale's 2024 identification in the cold case.
Victims provided impact statements during sentencing; Gale's attorney filed an appeal.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla, Boston Celtics coach, dismisses the NBA Coach of the Year award as 'stupid' and credits the team's success to players and staff.
Celtics have achieved the fourth-best basketball record, clinched a fifth-consecutive 50-win season, and are second in the Eastern Conference.
Mazzulla emphasizes his coaching focuses more on personal development and collaboration rather than basketball achievements or personal accolades.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person was killed in a car crash in Mattapan late Sunday night, according to Boston Police.
The incident occurred at 11:14 p.m. on American Legion Highway at Walk Hill Street and Canterbury Street.
The victim was found deceased at the scene with extensive damage to the front and driver side of the car.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds for NBA Coach of the Year, behind Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff, with +155 odds.
The Celtics achieved a strong record despite key player absences, showcasing player development and ranking in the league's top-four for both offense and defense.
The NBA Coach of the Year award, named after Red Auerbach, hasn't been won by a Celtics coach since Bill Fitch in 1980.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An 'End ICE' ice sculpture was vandalized after Boston's 'No Kings' rally, with vandals destroying the 24-foot artwork.
The sculpture, carved by artist Kat Dressler, was designed to maintain its message even if targeted by vandals.
Boston police were dispatched, but no report filed; a sign promoting a progressive group was also damaged.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, was sentenced to 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting three minors, including a 6-year-old girl.
Merced pleaded guilty to federal charges of sexual exploitation of a minor and possession of child pornography in July 2025.
Merced's federal sentence will run concurrently with a 25-30 year state prison term for related offenses.
